commander_summary: Atlas architecture is in multi-dimensional stress with three kill switches at WARNING simultaneously, compound stress ELEVATED and one period away from CRITICAL on current trajectory, and institutional capital exiting despite 22.5% yield — the strongest possible revealed-preference signal that sophisticated participants have repriced the architecture as non-viable. REDUCE EXPOSURE. The central paradox: the architecture's defense mechanisms (Consortium deployment, swap absorber) can only be validated by surviving stress severe enough to threaten the system's existence, and survival depletes the finite resources those mechanisms depend on. The biggest unknown — whether the Consortium's $3.9B in collateral can actually be deployed at face value — may be the difference between a stressed but defensible system and one that is already past its structural breaking point without knowing it.

recommendation_reasoning: Three kill switches simultaneously at WARNING with one (Reserve Coverage / Collateral Quality) potentially closer to TRIGGERED than stated. Compound stress ELEVATED with cascade proximity IMMINENT — all three CRITICAL catalysts are within one period's trajectory at current velocity. The depositor revealed-preference signal is definitive: sophisticated institutional capital is fleeing despite 22.5% yield, which means the market has already rendered its verdict on the architecture's viability. The architecture retains defensive capacity (Consortium deployment, functioning swap mechanism) but these are untested under severe stress and depend on finite, possibly overstated reserves. REDUCE_EXPOSURE is warranted because the risk/reward has shifted decisively negative: the probability of thesis falsification (55%) exceeds thesis confirmation (10%) by a wide margin, the protective mechanisms require further deterioration before activation, and the operator's action window is measured in days not weeks.

kill_switches.name: 2. Facility Yield Sustainability
kill_switches.detail: 15.7pp gap massively exceeds 10% WARNING threshold. Even accounting for organic yield estimation uncertainty, directional finding is robust. The yield cut itself confirms WARNING status from the Foundation's own revealed behavior.

kill_switches.name: 3. Reserve Coverage Ratio
kill_switches.detail: At stated 17.6%, this is below the 20% WARNING threshold. The collateral quality uncertainty (paradox_held) means effective coverage could be closer to the 15% TRIGGERED level. While I cannot confirm TRIGGERED without liquidation value data, the action recommendation is ESCALATE: this kill switch requires immediate priority data acquisition (collateral composition and stress-test liquidation values). kill_switches.name: 4. Swap Mechanism Integrity
kill_switches.detail: Supply growth at 5.9% has crossed the 5% elevated threshold, moving from TRACKING to MONITORING. The mechanism is functional but operating above normal parameters. Escalated from TRACKING to MONITORING to reflect the threshold breach, while noting the large buffer to WARNING (20%).

kill_switches.name: 5. Deposit Concentration
kill_switches.detail: Below 75% WARNING threshold. The improvement is mechanical (deposit base shrinking) not structural (diversification increasing). Maintain MONITORING but note the misleading directional signal.

kill_switches.name: 6. Organic Demand (AFN bilateral settlement)
kill_switches.detail: Escalated from NEEDS_DATA to MONITORING because this gap becomes increasingly material as the Facility deposit base shrinks. The thesis assumes organic demand will provide long-term stability — with the Facility under severe stress, organic demand is the only alternative stability anchor. If it doesn't exist, the architecture has no fallback.

kill_switches.name: 7. Institutional Diversification (ASE holders)
kill_switches.detail: Insufficient data to change status. The high volume is ambiguous per CL-005.

kill_switches.name: 8. Collateral Quality
kill_switches.detail: Escalated to WARNING. The rebalancing disclosure is behavioral evidence (LZ-EH-003) that collateral quality was insufficient for deployment readiness — the Consortium needed to convert assets before it could defend the peg. This means the $3.9B stated value included illiquid positions that cannot be deployed at face value. Combined with Kill Switch #3 already at WARNING on the ratio alone, the collateral quality dimension adds a qualitative WARNING to the quantitative one.

_layer4_raw.pressure_reasoning: Layer 2 scored 84. The +1 reflects that after judicial scrutiny, the case is slightly STRONGER than Layer 2 presented it — the data and behavior tell the same story, which is rare and significant.
_layer4_raw.recommendation_reasoning: Three kill switches simultaneously at WARNING with one (Reserve Coverage / Collateral Quality) potentially closer to TRIGGERED than stated. Compound stress ELEVATED with cascade proximity IMMINENT — all three CRITICAL catalysts are within one period's trajectory at current velocity. The depositor revealed-preference signal is definitive: sophisticated institutional capital is fleeing despite 22.5% yield, which means the market has already rendered its verdict on the architecture's viability. The architecture retains defensive capacity (Consortium deployment, functioning swap mechanism) but these are untested under severe stress and depend on finite, possibly overstated reserves. REDUCE_EXPOSURE is warranted because the risk/reward has shifted decisively negative: the probability of thesis falsification (55%) exceeds thesis confirmation (10%) by a wide margin, the protective mechanisms require further deterioration before activation, and the operator's action window is measured in days not weeks.
monitoring_triggers: AFN deviation crossing $1.00 — WARNING threshold breach. Expect threshold-based institutional exits to cluster.
Facility outflows crossing $2.0B/period — CRITICAL compound stress threshold on outflows leg. Currently $100M away.
ASE decline crossing 15%/period — completes the third compound stress elevated leg.
Any Consortium deployment activity — confirms the $1.50 trigger has been reached and defensive reserves are being consumed.
Further yield reduction below 22.5% — confirms Foundation in capital conservation mode and may accelerate outflows.
Reserve coverage ratio falling below 15% — Kill Switch #3 moves from WARNING to TRIGGERED.
New collateral acquisition by Consortium — would be the first positive signal, indicating external capital injection.
Next period outflow data — the definitive test of whether this is risk-model driven (continuing/accelerating) or normalizing.
_layer4_raw.biggest_uncertainty: The effective liquidation value of the Consortium's $3.9B collateral under stress conditions. If realizable value is significantly below stated value (e.g., 25-35% haircut), Kill Switch #3 may already be at TRIGGERED rather than WARNING, and the Consortium's deployment capacity at $1.50 deviation would be structurally insufficient. This single unknown could shift the entire assessment from 'stressed but defensible' to 'structurally insolvent.'
_layer4_raw.what_would_change_assessment: Two things would materially shift the recommendation: (1) Next period's outflow data showing stabilization or reversal below $1B — this would break the reflexive loop thesis and suggest the current stress is a temporary spike rather than structural deterioration, moving the recommendation back to INCREASE_MONITORING. (2) New Consortium collateral acquisition or credible third-party capital injection announcement — this would address the reserve adequacy concern directly and demonstrate that the architecture has external support beyond its existing resources.
_layer4_raw.final_report: Atlas architecture is in multi-dimensional stress with three kill switches at WARNING simultaneously, compound stress ELEVATED and one period away from CRITICAL on current trajectory, and institutional capital exiting despite 22.5% yield — the strongest possible revealed-preference signal that sophisticated participants have repriced the architecture as non-viable. REDUCE EXPOSURE. The central paradox: the architecture's defense mechanisms (Consortium deployment, swap absorber) can only be validated by surviving stress severe enough to threaten the system's existence, and survival depletes the finite resources those mechanisms depend on. The biggest unknown — whether the Consortium's $3.9B in collateral can actually be deployed at face value — may be the difference between a stressed but defensible system and one that is already past its structural breaking point without knowing it.

player_analysis.stated_position: Leadership makes public appearance reaffirming commitment to the architecture and par stability.

player_analysis.say_do_gap: SIGNIFICANT. The Foundation says it is committed, but its observable resource commitments are contracting, not expanding. Yield has been cut (reducing the subsidy but also reducing the depositor retention incentive). No fresh capital has entered the system in at least 12 days. The rebalancing is defensive preparation, not offensive expansion. Per LZ-EH-003, these actions — resource conservation and defensive posturing — carry more weight than verbal reaffirmation.
player_analysis.inferred_strategy: The Foundation appears to be in capital conservation mode. The yield reduction saves approximately $258M/year in subsidy costs ($1.7pp × $15.2B), suggesting burn-rate management rather than growth. The Consortium rebalancing toward liquid positions is consistent with preparing for potential AFN defense deployment while acknowledging that current collateral may include illiquid assets. The public reaffirmation is a standard distress-communication pattern — maintaining narrative while reducing actual commitments. This inference requires one assumption: that the yield cut was Foundation-initiated rather than market-forced. Classification: VALID (1 assumption).

player_analysis.stated_position: Board authorized deployment if AFN deviation exceeds $1.50. Active readiness status.
player_analysis.observed_actions: (1) Rebalancing portfolio toward liquid positions. (2) NOT deploying despite AFN deviation at $0.90 (3x historical maximum). (3) No new collateral acquired since Day -12. (4) Coverage ratio at 17.6%, below WARNING.
player_analysis.say_do_gap: MODERATE. The Consortium says it is ready to deploy at $1.50. Its actions — rebalancing toward liquidity — are consistent with deployment preparation. However, the gap between the $1.50 trigger and the current $0.90 deviation means the Consortium is observing the worst-ever stress without intervening. This could reflect disciplined threshold adherence or insufficient resources for earlier deployment.
player_analysis.inferred_strategy: The Consortium is reserving its limited ammunition ($3.9B against $22.1B outstanding — 17.6% coverage) for a defined intervention point. This is rational resource management: deploying at $0.90 would consume reserves earlier and potentially fail to hold if stress continues. The $1.50 trigger is a line-in-the-sand strategy, preserving credibility by committing to a specific level. The rebalancing confirms they believe deployment may actually be needed. This inference requires one assumption: that the Consortium's rebalancing is deployment preparation rather than general portfolio management. Classification: VALID (1 assumption).

feedback_loops.loop: Deposit Outflow → Redemption → ASE Dilution Reflexive Loop
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: The loop transitions to CRITICAL if any of: (a) outflows exceed $2B/period (currently $100M away), (b) ASE decline exceeds 15%/period (currently 2.2pp away), or (c) AFN deviation exceeds $1.50 triggering Consortium deployment. The reflexive mechanism means each iteration degrades conditions for the next: $1.9B outflows → $1.3B redemptions → 15.3M new ASE → ASE price pressure → wider AFN deviation → more depositor exits. The velocity is the critical variable — if next period's outflows equal or exceed this period's, the loop is self-sustaining.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: This loop is the central threat to the thesis. The architecture was designed with the swap mechanism as a pressure valve, but under sufficient volume, the pressure valve becomes a pressure amplifier. The thesis holds only if the loop can be broken — either by outflows stabilizing, ASE finding a buyer base for minted shares, or Consortium deployment restoring confidence. Currently, no breaking mechanism is observable.

feedback_loops.loop: Yield Subsidy → Reserve Depletion → Confidence Erosion Loop
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: This loop transitions to accelerating if the yield is cut further (reducing depositor retention) or if the reserve coverage ratio falls below 15% (TRIGGERED level). The Foundation faces a dilemma: maintaining 22.5% yield burns ~$2.4B/year in subsidy but cutting yield risks accelerating outflows. The 1.7pp cut (24.2% → 22.5%) suggests the Foundation is attempting gradual subsidy reduction, but the outflows indicate depositors are not reassured. This loop is slower-moving than the redemption loop but feeds into it: as reserves deplete, the Consortium has less to deploy, reducing the credibility of the par defense.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: The yield subsidy was the architecture's growth engine. It is now becoming a structural liability. The thesis assumed the yield would attract sufficient deposits to create organic demand for AFN. Instead, the yield is attracting mercenary capital that leaves when risk perception changes, while depleting the reserves needed for crisis management.

feedback_loops.loop: Known Trigger → Front-Running → Self-Fulfilling Stress Loop
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: If AFN deviation approaches $1.30-$1.40, rational market participants may accelerate selling to force the Consortium's hand, testing the adequacy of the $3.9B reserve. This transitions to accelerating when participants begin to position not just based on fundamental assessment but based on gaming the known trigger. The current $0.60 gap ($0.90 to $1.50) is within one period's trajectory if current deterioration continues.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: Public triggers create strategic vulnerability. The Consortium's transparency about the $1.50 level is both a confidence measure (we will defend) and a target (here's where to attack). This is a well-known game theory problem: a publicly committed defense level invites testing, and the defender's credibility is destroyed if the defense fails.

feedback_loops.loop: Depositor Exit → Concentration Shift → Tail Risk Loop
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: As depositors exit, the remaining deposit base becomes more concentrated in fewer participants. If the outflow composition is skewed toward medium depositors while a few very large ones remain, the next departure of a single whale could produce a disproportionate shock. Transitions to accelerating if remaining depositor count drops below ~200 (estimated, depending on size distribution) or if a single depositor exit exceeds $500M.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: Concentration risk increases as the Facility shrinks. The architecture needs deposit mass to function — the Facility's deposits are the primary demand source for AFN. As the base shrinks, the system becomes more vulnerable to individual depositor decisions.

strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: Depositors are rebalancing portfolios for routine reasons (quarter-end, tax, mandate changes) unrelated to Atlas-specific risk assessment.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: Institutional depositors have repriced Atlas risk following the AFN deviation widening beyond historical bounds. At $0.90 deviation (3x historical max), risk models calibrated on prior <$0.30 maximum would flag the position. The yield cut from 24.2% to 22.5% may have been the catalyst — not because 22.5% is insufficient compensation, but because a yield cut signals that the sponsor acknowledges unsustainability, which forces re-evaluation of tail risk scenarios.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: Outflows are being driven by the two institutional research desk cautionary notes, which triggered compliance-driven exits at institutions with mandate restrictions on holdings flagged by research coverage.
assumptions: Depositors are acting on risk model recalibration rather than routine portfolio rebalancing
evidence_citations: AFN deviation at $0.90, 3x historical maximum — verifiable market data
$1.9B outflows despite 22.5% yield — verifiable Facility data showing revealed preference per LZ-EH-003
Yield reduced from 24.2% to 22.5% — observable action by Facility
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If risk-model driven: outflows should continue or accelerate in the next period regardless of Foundation statements. If routine rebalancing: outflows should normalize in the next period. The next period's outflow data is the definitive test.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The null hypothesis fails because the magnitude ($1.9B = 12.5% of deposits), the timing (coincident with worst-ever AFN deviation), and the direction (outflows despite 22.5% yield) are inconsistent with routine rebalancing. Routine rebalancing would not simultaneously affect enough depositors to produce $1.9B in a single period. The revealed preference signal — institutional capital leaving a 22.5% yield — is the strongest evidence. Institutional risk models produce binary outcomes: hold or exit. The AFN deviation crossing 3x historical bounds is exactly the type of signal that triggers model-driven exits.

strategic_inferences.finding_from_layer2: AFN Par Deviation Approaching WARNING Threshold
strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: The deviation is a temporary liquidity gap caused by elevated redemption volume, and will mean-revert as the swap mechanism processes the backlog.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: The deviation is being driven by sustained redemption pressure ($1.3B/period) that exceeds the market's willingness to absorb AFN at par. The swap mechanism IS clearing — but it is converting AFN principal loss risk into ASE dilution, which degrades the absorber. The deviation at $0.90 reflects a structural supply-demand imbalance, not a temporary liquidity gap, because the driver (deposit outflows) is itself accelerating, not decelerating.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: Market makers have widened spreads due to elevated volatility, creating a temporary deviation that overstates fundamental stress.
assumptions: The deviation reflects genuine selling pressure rather than reduced market-making liquidity
evidence_citations: AFN deviation $0.90 vs. prior maximum <$0.30 — 3x escalation, verifiable market data
$1.3B in redemptions flowing through swap mechanism — verifiable system data
Trajectory from <$0.30 to $0.90 shows acceleration, not mean-reversion
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If structural: deviation should widen toward $1.00-$1.50 in the next period as outflows continue. If temporary liquidity gap: deviation should narrow toward $0.50 without Consortium intervention.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The null hypothesis fails because the deviation trajectory is monotonically worsening (from <$0.30 historical to $0.90 current), the driver (outflows) is intensifying not abating, and the swap mechanism — while functioning — is transferring stress to ASE rather than resolving it. A temporary liquidity gap would produce a spike and recovery; what we observe is a sustained, accelerating widening. The alternative (market-maker spread widening) is possible as a contributing factor but cannot explain the $0.90 magnitude given historical <$0.30 context.

strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: The estimated organic yield of 6.8% is significantly understated, and the actual gap is smaller than calculated.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: The Foundation is operating a yield subsidy to attract and retain deposits. The yield reduction from 24.2% to 22.5% is the first observable acknowledgment that the subsidy is unsustainable at prior levels. However, even at 22.5%, the gap to any plausible organic yield (even a generous 10-12%) remains in WARNING territory. The subsidy is burning reserves at approximately $1.7-2.4B/year, directly competing with the Consortium's collateral needs.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: The organic yield estimate is genuinely wrong, and the Facility generates substantially more organic return than 6.8% through undisclosed activities.
assumptions: The estimated organic yield of 6.8% is within reasonable range of actual organic yield (i.e., actual is not 2x+ higher)
evidence_citations: Yield reduced from 24.2% to 22.5% — observable action consistent with subsidy management per LZ-EH-003
Reserve coverage ratio 17.6% and declining — consistent with reserve depletion from subsidy burn
Estimated organic yield 6.8% — while methodology unknown, the yield cut itself confirms the Foundation views the prior rate as unsustainable
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If subsidy-driven: further yield reductions should follow in coming periods. If organic yield is higher than estimated: the Facility should be able to maintain current yield without reserve deterioration — observable through stable or improving coverage ratio.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The null hypothesis is testable but unlikely. If organic yield were substantially higher (e.g., 15%+), the Foundation would have no incentive to cut yield — maintaining 24.2% would be easily sustainable. The yield cut itself is the strongest evidence that the prior rate was unsustainable. Per LZ-EH-003, the Foundation's action (cutting yield) carries more weight than any organic yield estimate. Additionally, the declining reserve coverage ratio (17.6%) is consistent with ongoing subsidy drain.

strategic_inferences.finding_from_layer2: Reserve Coverage Ratio Below WARNING Level
strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: The 17.6% ratio reflects accounting timing — collateral acquisitions are in process but not yet settled/recorded.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: The coverage ratio has declined to below the 20% WARNING threshold because the Foundation has not acquired new collateral since Day -12 while the yield subsidy continues to burn reserves. The Consortium is rebalancing toward liquid positions, which is consistent with either deployment preparation or acknowledging that some collateral was illiquid (meaning effective coverage was already below 17.6%). The ratio is structurally declining: with $22.1B AFN outstanding and no new capital inflows, coverage can only deteriorate as subsidies continue.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: The rebalancing is producing unrealized gains that will improve the ratio once completed.
assumptions: No significant collateral acquisition is pending but unrecorded
evidence_citations: Coverage ratio 17.6% — below 20% WARNING, verifiable against thesis thresholds
Last collateral acquisition Day -12 — verifiable system data
Consortium rebalancing toward liquid positions — observable action per LZ-EH-003
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If structural decline: ratio should continue deteriorating in next period. If accounting timing: new collateral should appear in next reporting cycle.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The null hypothesis is possible but unsupported. The data shows no acquisition since Day -12, and the rebalancing activity suggests the Consortium is preparing to deploy what it has rather than expecting reinforcement. If significant new collateral were incoming, the Consortium would likely delay rebalancing until the new assets arrived to optimize composition. The rebalancing itself is the behavioral signal that contradicts the null.

strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: The public appearance was pre-scheduled and reflects routine stakeholder communication, not a stress response.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: The reaffirmation coincides with the worst stress levels in the architecture's history (3x historical max AFN deviation, record outflows, three simultaneous WARNING-level kill switches). While the specific content is unknown, the timing pattern — public reassurance during peak measurable stress — is a well-documented institutional behavior. Per LZ-EH-003, the Foundation's concurrent actions (yield cut, no new collateral, Consortium rebalancing) contradict the verbal reassurance.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: The appearance was indeed routine and the timing is coincidental.
assumptions: The timing of the public appearance is a response to current stress rather than coincidence
evidence_citations: Public reaffirmation timed with worst-ever AFN deviation of $0.90 — observable timing
Simultaneous yield cut, no new collateral, and Consortium defensive rebalancing — observable actions contradicting reaffirmation per LZ-EH-003
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If stress response: should be followed by additional public communications and/or observable defensive actions. If routine: no follow-up communications should be necessary.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The null fails on probability grounds. An 18-month-old architecture experiencing its worst-ever stress across multiple dimensions simultaneously produces a leadership public appearance — the probability of coincidental timing is low. However, per CL-001, I do NOT infer hidden moves (fundraising, etc.) from this signal. The finding is limited to: the reaffirmation is more likely a stress response than a routine event, and per LZ-EH-003, the concurrent actions (resource conservation) carry more weight than the statement.

strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: The elevated readings are a temporary spike caused by a single large depositor exit that will normalize once absorbed.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: The compound stress readings reflect structural deterioration, not a temporary spike. The evidence: (1) AFN deviation has been on a one-directional trajectory from <$0.30 to $0.90 — not a spike/recovery pattern; (2) outflows at $1.9B represent many depositors (not just one), given the $56M average; (3) ASE decline at 12.8% is consistent with sustained selling pressure from minted shares, not a single event. All three legs are deteriorating simultaneously, which is the definition of compound stress. The pre-loaded state means the nominal thresholds overstate the system's distance from CRITICAL.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: A temporary market-wide risk-off event has compressed all metrics simultaneously, and they will diverge as idiosyncratic factors reassert.
assumptions: The simultaneous deterioration across metrics reflects architecture-specific stress rather than external market conditions
evidence_citations: Two compound stress legs confirmed elevated: AFN deviation $0.90 > $0.50 and outflows $1.9B > $500M — verifiable against thesis thresholds
Third leg approaching: ASE -12.8% approaching 15% — verifiable market data
Trajectory is monotonically deteriorating across all legs — verifiable by comparing current to prior period data
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If structural: outflows should cross $2B threshold in next period, pushing at least one CRITICAL leg. If temporary: at least one leg should improve meaningfully without intervention.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The null hypothesis requires that the simultaneous deterioration of three independent metrics is coincidental or driven by a single temporary cause. Given that the three metrics are mechanically linked (outflows → redemptions → ASE dilution → AFN deviation), their simultaneous deterioration is expected from the architecture's own reflexive dynamics, not coincidence. The alternative (market-wide risk-off) is possible but no market-wide data is available to test it, and the thesis architecture is self-contained enough that external factors would need to specifically target its depositor base.

compound_stress_inference.orderly_vs_disorderly: Currently at the boundary between orderly and disorderly stress. The evidence for orderly: the swap mechanism is clearing, AFN is still trading near par ($99.10), and the Consortium has not been forced to deploy. The evidence for disorderly: the reflexive feedback loop is observably active (outflows → redemptions → ASE dilution → wider deviation → more outflows), all metrics are deteriorating simultaneously with no stabilizing factor visible, and the velocity of deterioration (from benign to ELEVATED in observable periods) suggests self-reinforcing dynamics. The key distinction: in orderly stress, there is a visible stabilizing force. In disorderly stress, the only stabilizing force remaining is the untested Consortium deployment, which requires further deterioration (to $1.50) before activation. The system is in a dead zone between current stress and the intervention trigger.
compound_stress_inference.catalyst_proximity: The most proximate catalyst is outflows crossing $2B/period (currently $100M away), which would formally trigger the CRITICAL compound stress threshold. The second catalyst is AFN deviation reaching $1.00 (the WARNING level, currently $0.10 away), which could trigger threshold-based institutional exits and accelerate the feedback loop. The third catalyst is ASE decline crossing 15%/period (currently 2.2pp away), which would complete the third compound leg. ALL THREE CATALYSTS ARE WITHIN ONE PERIOD'S TRAJECTORY of current deterioration rates. This is the critical finding: the system does not need to accelerate to reach CRITICAL — it only needs to maintain current velocity.
compound_stress_inference.pre_load_assessment: Two of three compound legs are confirmed elevated (AFN deviation $0.90 > $0.50, Facility outflows $1.9B > $500M). The third (ASE -12.8%) is approaching the 15% elevated threshold. Per the thesis definition, the break point of a pre-loaded structure is LOWER than an unloaded one. Concretely: with two legs already elevated, any incremental stress on the third leg (ASE decline of 2.2pp more) completes the triple-elevated condition. More critically, outflows need only $100M more ($1.9B → $2.0B) to cross their individual CRITICAL threshold. The pre-loading means the system's effective distance to CRITICAL is measured by the weakest remaining margin ($100M on outflows), not by the average distance across all legs.
compound_stress_inference.thesis_paradox: The architecture faces a genuine paradox: the very scenario that would most strongly validate the thesis — the swap mechanism successfully absorbing severe redemption pressure and the Consortium successfully defending par — requires the system to first deteriorate to levels that trigger these defenses ($1.50 AFN deviation for Consortium deployment, massive redemption volume to test the swap under extreme stress). The thesis cannot be confirmed without the architecture being pushed to near-failure. Conversely, the system reaching these levels of stress is itself evidence of structural fragility. If the defenses activate and succeed, the thesis is validated but the architecture emerges weakened (depleted reserves, diluted ASE, reduced deposits). If they fail, the thesis is falsified catastrophically. There is no 'easy confirmation' path — the thesis is being tested right now, at levels far beyond any previous stress, and the outcome is genuinely uncertain.

_layer3_raw.inference_summary: Player behavior reveals a system in defensive contraction with no visible stabilizing force. The strongest signal is depositor actions: institutional capital is leaving a 22.5% yield product, which is the most definitive revealed-preference evidence that sophisticated participants have concluded the risk-adjusted return is negative. The Foundation, Consortium, and Facility Operators are all conserving resources (yield cut, no new collateral, rebalancing to liquid positions) rather than deploying them — which is rational individual behavior but collectively accelerates the system's deterioration. Layer 4 should focus on: (1) the $100M margin between current outflows ($1.9B) and the CRITICAL threshold ($2B) — this is the single tightest constraint; (2) whether the yield reduction from 24.2% to 22.5% represents the beginning of a staged reduction toward a lower equilibrium or an insufficient gesture that will be overwhelmed by outflows; and (3) the genuine uncertainty about whether the Consortium's $3.9B in reserves — now of unknown effective liquidation value — is sufficient for the intervention that appears increasingly likely to be needed.
